title: 2-step word problems
description: Solve two-step word problems with addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.  Some questions include estimation.
topic_path: Module 3: Multiplication and division with units of 0, 1, 6–9, and multiples of 10
Topic E: Analysis of patterns and problem solving including units of 0 and 1

assessment_items.question_content:
I put $75$ jelly beans in a bowl. Each day I eat $6$ jelly beans.
 
**How many jelly beans  are in the bowl after $9$ days?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 1]] jelly beans
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]






I eat $6$ jelly beans each day for $9$ days.

We can multiply to find the number of jelly beans eaten. 

$9\times6=\blueD{54}$

$\greenD{\text{Total}}$ jelly beans $-$ jelly beans $\blueD{\text{eaten}}$ $=$ number  left in the $\maroonD{\text{bowl}}$ 



$\greenD{75}-\blueD{54}=\maroonD{21}$
$\maroonD{21}$ jelly beans are in the bowl after $9$ days.

assessment_items.question_content:
I used $9$ sheets of toilet paper each day for $7$ days. The roll had $96$ sheets when it was new. 
 
**How many sheets of toilet paper  have not been used?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] sheets
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]



I used $9$ sheets of toilet paper each day for $7$ days.

We can multiply to find the number of sheets used.
 
I have used $7\times9=\blueD{63}$ sheets.

$\greenD{\text{Total}}$ sheets of paper $-$ sheets of paper $\blueD{\text{used}}$ $=$ number of sheets left on the $\maroonD{\text{roll}}$ 



$\greenD{96}-\blueD{63}=\maroonD{33}$
$\maroonD{33}$ sheets have not been used.

assessment_items.question_content:
It costs $\$120$ to have a party for $9$ people at the skating rink. The cost includes a charge of $\$10$ for each person attending the party plus the price of a cake.

**How much does the cake cost?**    

$\$$ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]

There are $9$ people at the party and each paid $\$10$.

We can multiply to find the cost for the people at the party. 

The cost for the party guests is $9\times\$10=\$\blueD{90}$.

$\greenD{\text{Total}}$ cost of the party $-$ costs of the $\blueD{\text{guests}}$ $=$ cost of the  $\maroonD{\text{cake}}$. 



$\greenD{120}-\blueD{90}=\maroonD{30}$
The cake is $\$\maroonD{30}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
My book is $131$ pages. I have been reading $7$ pages each day for $8$ days.

**How many pages are left to read?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] pages
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]


I have read $7$ pages each day for $8$ days.

We can multiply to find the number of pages read so far.

$8\times7=\blueD{56}$

$\greenD{\text{Total}}$ pages $-$ pages $\blueD{\text{read}}$ $=$ pages $\maroonD{\text{left}}$ to read

$\greenD{131}-\blueD{56}=\maroonD{75}$
$\maroonD{75}$ pages are left to read.

assessment_items.question_content:
Our skating club is going on a field trip. To cover the cost of the trip we are collecting $\$8$ from each of the $9$ members of the club. We also raised $\$178$ at a car wash. 

**How much money have we raised for our trip all together?**
  
$\$$ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]



$9$ members each paid $\$8$.

We can multiply to find the amount collected from members.   

$9\times8=\$\blueD{72}$

Amount from $\blueD{\text{members}}$ $+$ amount from $\maroonD{\text{car wash}}$ $=$ $\greenD{\text{total money raised}}$

$\blueD{72} + \maroonD{178}= \greenD{250}$
We have raised $\$250$ for our trip.

assessment_items.question_content:
I have $47$ marbles. Then, $6$ of my friends each give me $7$ marbles.
 
**How many marbles  do I have?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] marbles
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]





$6$ of my friends each give me $7$ marbles.

We can multiply to find the total number of marbles from friends. 

$6\times7=\blueD{42}$

Marbles $\blueD{\text{from friends}}$ $+$  $\maroonD{\text{starting}}$ marbles $=$ $\greenD{\text{total}}$ marbles
 
$\blueD{42}+\maroonD{47}=\greenD{89}$
I have $89$ marbles.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mabel had a skating rink party for her birthday. The cost to rent the party room was $\$75$.  The cost of the ticket for each of the $6$ people who came to the party was $\$8$.

**What was the total cost of the party?**  

$\$$ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]


$6$ people each paid $8$ for a ticket.

We can multiply to find the total cost of the tickets. 

$6\times8=\blueD{48}$

Cost of  $\blueD{\text{tickets}}$ $+$ cost of  $\maroonD{\text{party room}}$ $=$ $\greenD{\text{total}} $ cost

$\blueD{48} +\maroonD{75} = \greenD{123}$
The total cost of the party was $\$123$.

assessment_items.question_content:
I had a jar of jelly beans that weighed $56$ ounces.  I added $16$ more ounces of jelly beans to the jar. Then I divided the jelly beans evenly into bags that each weighed $8$ ounces.
 
**How many bags of jelly beans did I make?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] bags
hints:
We can add to find the total amount of jelly beans.  

$56+16=72$ ounces

Now we can divide the total ounces into groups with $8$ ounces  in each group.

$72 \div 8 = 9$
I made $9$ bags of jelly beans.

assessment_items.question_content:
I had $42$ ounces of rice. On Monday I bought $58$ more ounces of rice. Then I divided all the rice into $10$ equal-sized portions for dinner with friends.
 
**How many ounces  of rice were in each portion?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] ounces
hints:
We can add to find the total weight of the rice.  

$42+58=100$ ounces

Now we can divide the total amount of rice into $10$ equal-sized groups.

$100 \div 10 = 10$
Each portion had $10$ ounces of rice.

assessment_items.question_content:
I had $\$57$. Then I earned $\$15$ babysitting. I spent all of the money to buy $9$ movie tickets.  
 
**How much did each movie ticket cost?**  


$\$$ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
I can add to find the amount of money I had all together.

$57+15=$$\$72$

Now we can divide the total amount of money  into $9$ equal-sized groups.

$72\div9=8$
Each movie ticket cost $\$8$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Our school library started the day with $284$ books. During the day, $28$ books were returned, and some others were checked out. At the end of the day, the school library had $293$ books.  
 
**How many books  were checked out today?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] books
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]



If no books were checked out, the library would have $284+28=312$ books at the end of the day.
$\pink{19}$ books were checked out.

assessment_items.question_content:
When I woke up, it was $37$ degrees. During the day, the temperature increased $14$ degrees to the hottest temperature of the day. When I went to bed, it was $29$ degrees.  
 
**How much did the temperature decrease between the hottest point in the day and when I went to bed?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] degrees
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]

The high temperature was $37+14=51$ degrees.
The temperature decreased $\pink{22}$ degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
The cash register at the ice cream store started the day with $\$120$. During the day, the store earned $\$56$ selling ice cream.  The manager also paid for a delivery out of the cash register. At the end of the day, the cash register had $\$141$.  
 
**How much did the delivery cost?**  

$\$$ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]

If no deliveries were paid, the cash register would have $120+56=176$ dollars at the end of the day.
The cost of the delivery was $\$\pink{35}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The oven temperature was at $325$ degrees because my mom just finished baking a pie. I increased the temperature $75$ degrees so I could bake a pizza. After the pizza was cooked, I turned off the oven. The oven cooled to the room temperature of $68$ degrees. 
 
**How much did the temperature  decrease?**  

$$[[☃ input-number 1]] degrees
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]



The high temperature was $325+75=400$.
The temperature decreased $\maroonD{332}$ degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
My book is $79$ pages. I have been reading $10$ pages each day. There are $9$ pages left to read. 

**How many days have I been reading the book?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] days
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]



I have read $79-9=70$ pages.
I have been reading the book for $\pink{7}$ days.

assessment_items.question_content:
The total cost for my brother's bowling party was $\$140$.  It cost $\$50$ to reserve a bowling lane plus the cost of renting shoes for the $9$ people attending.  

**How much is the cost  of renting shoes per person?**  

$\$$ [[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]



The total additional cost for the group of $9$ people is $140-50=90$ dollars.
The cost for each person is $\$\pink{10}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
I have $28$ marbles. If I divide the marbles into bags of $8$ marbles, there will be $4$ marbles left over. 
 
**How many bags of $8$ marbles can I make?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] bags
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]



I can put $28-4=24$ marbles into bags of $8$.
I can make $\pink{3}$ bags of $8$ marbles.

assessment_items.question_content:
I put  $50$ sheets of paper in my binder $8$ days ago. I used the same number of sheets each day. Now I only have $2$ sheets left. 

**How many sheets of paper did I use each day?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] sheets
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]



I used $50-2=48$ sheets.
I used $\pink6$ sheets each day.

assessment_items.question_content:
I put $60$ jelly beans in a bowl $7$ days ago. Now there are $4$ jelly beans left. 
 
**If I ate the same number of jelly beans each day, how many jelly beans  did I eat each day?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] jelly beans
hints:
[[☃ image 1]]



I have eaten $60-4=56$ jelly beans.
If I ate the same number each day, I ate $\pink{8}$ jelly beans each day.
